none.justification: Answer A gets the subsystem split right for the few sites it found, but is badly incomplete: it claims USE_JEMALLOC is confined to src/debug.c and src/zmalloc.c, missing many #if defined(USE_JEMALLOC) sites across other src files. It also undercounts HAVE_BACKTRACE as 10 instead of 15 and misses the Makefile jemalloc build/link effect.

ccodegraph.justification: Answer B correctly classifies the listed sites, but falsely says the grep returns exactly 14 lines and no Makefile gating, contradicting the GT. It misses most USE_JEMALLOC sites, undercounts HAVE_BACKTRACE, and omits the required jemalloc build-system effect.

lsp.justification: Answer C recognizes that literal #ifdef sites are not the whole USE_JEMALLOC story and names the other jemalloc-gated files with the right allocator classification. It still undercounts HAVE_BACKTRACE as 10 rather than 15 and misses the src/Makefile jemalloc build-system effect, so it has real GT-scoring gaps.
